title: scleria swamyi
intro: Scleria swamyi, also known as the Scleria grass, is a species of sedge that belongs to the family Cyperaceae. It is an aquatic plant commonly found in wetland ecosystems, particularly in India and Sri Lanka. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Scleria swamyi include treating fever, diarrhea, and skin conditions, and its herbal preparations include infusions, decoctions, and poultices made from its leaves, roots, and rhizomes.
